reviews.review_text:
Personal Rating: Very Positive (85%)

I got this game while the 2025 Summer sale, and then shelved it for a year. But now I came back to it, so here are my thoughts.

Story: It seems like another game where you have to survive the end of humanity, nothing really special in this genre. I still don't know if you can just free roam after getting on the surface, but I guess I will figure it out.

Character creator is very good, and better than Code Vein 2's Character Creator Demo, it's much more readable.

Gameplay: It is incomparable to something like AI Limit and definitely not Dark Souls difficulty. Code Vein is very easy compared to these 2 games, pretty much effortless.

Negatives: Oversized weapons. They look visually great, but the hitboxes of them don't seem to align properly, so sometimes it seems like your weapon is just phasing through enemies, dealing 0 damage.

I would go for it when it's on sale (which it is pretty frequently, especially on the big ones)

okay so I've had time to actually think about this one, and the more I sit with it, the better it gets.
The vibes are genuinely immaculate, post-apocalyptic anime vampire aesthetic goes crazy hard, and the memories is actually one of the smartest storytelling mechanics I've seen in the genre. Like they built the entire narrative delivery around amnesia as a theme. You piece together who these people were through blood codes and lost memories and it clicks in a way that most soulslikes never even attempt. The writing carries this game on its back and doesn't complain once.
The companions are written beautifully - incredible backstories, real tragedies, actual thematic weight. the cutscenes do the heavy lifting and they do it well. You just wish the base had more life to it, more moments between the big story beats.
The gift system is basically a vending machine transaction right now. Which stings a little when the emotional foundation is this strong, imagine fallout style companion progression, reactions to your choices, dialogue that shifts based on your bond. the material is right there.
But none of that changes what this game actually is. it's peak.

This game is a compilation of every popular souls-like, except each piece is poorly done for my taste. Feels blank, plays okayish, gets awfully boring and repetitive since the game has huge difficulty spikes on each new location you get to, making you grind an enemy to get stuff from them, otherwise everyone is a sponge for hits. Bosses did not have any depth to them, some of which felt really out of place. I recon if the game got rid of the idea of being a souls-like, adding difficulty levels and adjusting areas a little, or at the least making the area navigation a little bit more straightforward, it would be a great

It's an easy and simplified souls-like with anime.  It doesn't do anything particularly well, and the level design (especially discount Anor Londo) is worthy of mockery, but the total package manages to be fun regardless.
At first, the game feels promising, but the exploration quickly becomes frustrating. The map design is confusing, poorly made, and feels like random mazes stitched together with repetitive scenery.

It's just a rough game. The story does have some good points more nearing the end but enemys, levels and gear can old real fast as everything is similar. Characters just fall flat only like two characters the game even tries to get you care about, but it just dosen't do a good job. An lastly biggest thing the gameplay feels rough as hell weapons don't feel great and how you move while using them even worse. Code vain tries alot but it just comes off flat, bland and just so close to be an okay game.

CODE VEIN,  a souls like for gooners.
 If you are a person who loves a lot of exposition in story, this game is for you. 
Gameplay is decent; however the art style is the main draw to the game. 
If you are coming from godslayer, have fun! and don't buy 2!
8/10 would play again.
P.S--> Io is pretty

Code Vein hits a sour spot in its difficulty, at least for me, where it feels too easy for me easily immerse into the world, but it isnt hard enough for me to gain the soulsborne genre's accomplishment feeling. The level design is also riddled with labyrinthian design which makes exploration feel like a punishment if you dont know the proper path to follow, especially since there isnt much reward in exploring with how linear the game is.
combat feels a little clunky but thats it. i love everything else about this game. except the overpriced dlcs ofc. the musics are lit af
It's a bit boring, enemy collision is a pain in the arse and The Cathedral of The Sacred Blood is one of the worst sections of a game I've ever played.

I'm so glad I bought this on an 85% off discount cuz Hooooooly **** is this game not worth it's full price. This thing FEELS like it's still in early access. The controls are constantly fighting me, inputs don't always read (I checked, it's not my controller) and the STUNLOCK...... if the devs had spent even a quarter of the amount of time they spent on the cutscenes on the combat and movement instead, this would be a solid game. And from what I hear about the sequel they haven't learned their lesson.

Do yourself a favor and play AI Limit or actual DarkSouls instead of this pile of poopoo.
A barebones souls-like with clunky combat and an abysmally boring story. If you aren't used to other souls-likes, maybe give it a try but after playing so many this just feels shallow.

if you just want a fun hack and slash with some anime elements, this is worth a try.

anything more than that and you're kind of reaching. exploration is quite fun, but eventually it feels repetitive. i like that they give you a map that constantly updates so they have more freedom to just make finding paths way harder, but eventually you're just repeating the same thing again and again. 

there are four enemies in this game, outside of the bosses. slime, dog, person, larger person. no seriously.

the build system with the blood codes is quite fun. i feel like my experience with soulslikes made this game a touch too easy, but otherwise it was fine. 

i can't say much about the story, though the premise was interesting. unfortunately, for some reason the game thinks it's a film and every shot lingers about 3 seconds more than it should on the cinematic cutscenes. the way the story is delivered is so slow and tedious that i just started skipping everything halfway through the game. i get that it's a jrpg, and outside the cutscenes reading through dialogue is absolutely fine (entirely because once i'm done reading a line i can skip to the next line without having to wait for them to finish speaking it, which is what you'd normally expect). but in the cutscenes themselves it plods on too much, and there's too many cutscenes.

i personally don't find the characters too interesting, but i can't expect too much.

bossfights are okay, if not repetitive. you kinda fight them all the same way. which can still be fun of course.

Caveat to the positive review: Buy it on sale. I would not recommend the game at full price.

I have a lot of gripes with this game, such as:
-Lack of enemy variety
-Lack of actually needing to experiment with / switch between builds
-Untelegraphed or too-precise dodge windows for some attacks
-Overly long and vertical areas which are frustrating to traverse (usually for little benefit from what you find)
-A plot that explains itself a little bit too much for my liking, reducing the mystery and opening itself up to inconsistency / oversimplification
-Lack of body language and emotion outside rendered cutscenes which lends to some dissonance with the emotional dialogue

But, all that said, I can't call the game bad. The characters and mystery (while it lasts) of what's happening really carry the game, and aside from the aforementioned lacking aspects, the gameplay *is* fun at its core. If you can put up with it being a bit unrefined and some annoyances of enemy placement, it's well worth probably $15-30 dependent on your affinity for Soulslikes this many years after release.

It's good, but at same time it's really bad. Most locations are labyrinths, near the end there's pointless shortcuts, some bosses feel absolutely unfair, final boss feels like it's 100% random, but at same time this game has quite good story (i liked it), some bosses are really good and look cool.
Play it only if you really want to and if it's on really good discount.
This game is a souls-like with the proper atmosphere and environment in my opinion. Less jump-scare logic, more story content. Great bridge.

different game with doomsday anime world that u can love it if u are an anime fan. 
worth it much on offers.
Pretty good souls-like. The story did not connect with me much, but the game play was good. I did like the different classes you can play without investing into them.

Easier side for the souls-like genre, suitable for those that wanted to try this genre but dont want to sweat it out too hard.

mechanically nothing too special, maybe only the parry system that quite interesting. most bosses was generics, only few are so hard but thats what make it good for first timer: not too challenging but need to still cautious because few hits guarantee a death. Artstyle also lovely since its more like an anime rather than photorealistics, adding more identity to the game.

character creation is the best thing in this game. so diverse style and customization with in depth detail, followed with story and soundtrack is where its shine, story wise its so good and tied perfectly with God Eater, soundtracks mostly are S tiers. 

definitely a buy on a discount.

I had no expectations, but this game pleasantly surprised in many ways. It was my first actual 3D Souls-like, and I had a ton of fun with it. I really enjoy the ease of experimentation with the Blood Code system, even if it is somewhat limited at the start. Despite a few sharp difficulty spikes I had heard about and finally experienced (a certain poison-spewing lady and a labirynth of marble), I enjoyed overcoming this game's challenges by taking my time and upgrading my character and weapons as best as I could at any given time as opposed to knocking my head against a wall. 

I also really enjoyed the story, atmosphere and characters, which took me by surprise as I have somewhat soured on anime and anime tropes over the years especially in videogames. Part of getting older, I guess. I really dug the companion system in this game, Yakumo, Io and Mia were definitely the MVPs.

I don't think I'll play it again after 100%'ing it, but I highly recommend it. it's an enjoyable, gory action packed game with tons to do. I didn't play online at all though. I'm the least multiplayer inclined gamer I know. Yes I would rather grind the damn Marks of Honor for hours (though it took considerably less than I expected) than have to deal with other people during my gaming time, thank you very much.

Code Vein is a solid game. It's not groundbreaking, but it's fun and it's well-made.

Code Vein is what you get if you combine Dark Souls general mechanics with a more Hack and Slash RPG. Code Vein uses a class system with its Blood Codes. Unlike other Souls-likes where the classes only influence starting the game, here the class will automatically manipulate your stats based on the class scaling. Each class has unique strengths and weaknesses. You can mix and match class skills once you have mastered them from either playing as that class or using items and the currency, Haze, to instantly master.

The pros of this class and leveling system are that you can play all classes and experiment with everything in 1 playthrough (except for a couple either or classes based on choices) and you don't have to worry about what stats to level as there are none that you can allocate points into. Instead you just have an overall level. The con is that this makes the game have less replayability.

The combat, depending on your build, is mid-to-fast paced and is generally engaging throughout the game. The story is sort of like a typical JRPG with interesting, if not expected, twists. The story is interesting but I would not buy this game for just the story. You really want it for the gameplay.

If you like Souls-like games and the anime/gothic aesthetic is interesting to you give this a try. Keep in mind this is on the edge of a Souls-like. If you only like Dark Souls this probably won't do it for you.

im gonna give this a thumbs up but i rlly wanna give this a mixed, i rlly like it but theres a few things that could be fixed. navigating can be a bit confusing and combat is challenging but its a souls-like so what can u do. story is... confusing. otherwise the design of the game is beautiful i like the idea of an anime souls-like and the world design is also jus gorgeous

USE A GUIDE FOR CATHREDAL, IT'S NOT WORTH IT. I did it blind and it was a waste of time.
In short, it's 'okay/neutral' overall, the combat and character customizer are the highlights, but the story was lame and long. The enemies forgettable. It's worth playing at $10 on sale maybe, but I wouldn't really recommend Code Vein. Bandai tried, they lack talented artists but it's not bad. If you temper your expectations and just enjoy the combat system, you can extract some hours of fun. It's a little rougher in the beginning and stumbles again at the big maze, otherwise solid until the end. Final Boss isn't very fun imo.

Good: 
-Map tracks your footsteps/exploration, lets you know where you haven't gone yet.
-Ability to sit in chairs, baths, beds, stools, etc. in the base (Firelink Shrine), play the piano, etc. I wish Fromsoft added some more passive actions to their hubs.
-Blood Code system & auto-leveling is interesting, you unlock classes as you play and can switch whenever for different scaling with weapons. Allows you a lot of freedom for builds.
-Combat mechanics are solid, dark souls rip-off but it works. No shields, just a block for chip damage.
-Insanely good character customization with a ton of patterns, placement options, clothing, hair, accessories, etc. This is the big hook imo, one of the best I've seen in a while. It has limits but it can do a LOT.
-Runs well, Fromslop games max 60fps, I can hold 144hz on high settings here which is nice.
-Lots of settings.
-Good ladder climb animations.
-Howling Pit was a notably good area, the balance of water and slowness, mist, enemies hiding, rewarding exploration, even the fallen city visuals were notably better than most levels.
-Plunging Attacks
-Menus, teleport, animations, everything in this regard is polished and works well.
-Companion voice lines have enough variation and dynamic triggers to not get repetitive, they will comment on environments, paths, enemies, story, etc. This is well done.

Neutral:
-Fan service, it was a negative for me but that's subjective. The boss that uses her staff as a stripper pole while half naked was where it really felt excessively forced.
-Blatantly ripping off DaS & DeS moments, didn't really bother me but it might annoy some.

Bad:
-The story is boring and delivered poorly, the memories are not very engaging and are viewed out of order unless you're very thorough and deliberate not to do this. The villains do some cringe anime stuff.
-The enemies have decent combat moves, but they're forgettable. The bosses are just bigger and similarly forgettable. I couldn't remember a name or what they looked like after. They need to design some more creative and shocking visuals, bosses should frighten and amaze. This is the big seller for these games.
-Levels have too much empty space. It's not egregious but you sometimes walk too far. Also why is sprint on RB, that took me a while to figure out as the tutorial didn't pop for me. Weird choice.
-Combat is not chunky. Swinging and hitting an enemy there's no feedback. I want some level of impact through audio design, a vibration, weapon slowdown, etc. as attacks don't feel powerful even when I'm dealing a ton of damage.
-Not particularly difficult, feel like I'm leveling fast enough to outpace enemies and if I want to hang back my companion can carry me. The DLC bosses like Hellfire Knight are actually pretty hard but I'm not sure if I can really consider that here as it's optional bonus content.
-Mouse keeps showing up in menus. These are controller games, this should be fixed.
-Most elevators only start to move after switching to 60fps. lol.
-Why are the achievements not hidden for every boss lol
-Too many gank areas, feels like DS2 at times. Having 6 enemies stacked up sucks, if you get clipped even once, you're stun locked infinitely into death.
-Hitboxes aren't the best, sometimes I'm swinging right through an enemy and it won't connect. Usually not an issue but needs cleaning up. There's a few attacks across the board that come out too fast without much of a tell, it's a bit unreasonable to react and you have to play around the possibility of it.
-Companions are a little strong, if you're low on heals you can hang back and let them solo enemies. I can also draw aggro and walk backwards while they do all the damage.

Personally, I thought the game was great. The graphics were solid for it's time, along with the fact that there are variety of weapons and blood veils to use, it's up to the player's discretion on how they want to take on the game. I loved the character creation plus the interactions with the crew, especially my waifu, Io. What I hated, was that she turned to stone, along with the iffy parries and swing animations that have gotten me killed. 9/10 would recommend.
Solid 7/10 game. Pretty fun, Blood Code system feels kind of pointless, but still a solid souls like.

My first souls-like game was Code Vein. I tried the demo first to get a feel for the gameplay mechanics, then bought it on sale afterward. Honestly, half of my playtime probably went into the character customization because it’s just that good 🔥.

During my first few hours, the gameplay felt really fun. There were lots of weapons to loot, the bosses were manageable for the most part (except that invading Executioner as my starter boss), and the Blood Code system made it interesting since you could freely experiment with different builds and playstyles.

As I progressed through the game, I slowly got hooked on both the story and the combat. Some fights were challenging, but the more you played, the easier it became to adapt to enemy attack patterns, except for Juzo Mido. Gotta admit it, I spent around 6 hours trying to beat him. After taking a break and coming back later, I finally defeated that mf.

The exploration was also pretty interesting, although some areas were confusing. The Cathedral especially felt like a Maze Runner map with all the twists, turns, and vertical paths. That area genuinely hurt my brain trying to figure out where to go next (seriously, who designed this map?). Even so, the game’s straightforward combat and progression kept the experience fun, challenging, and engaging.

Overall, I’d rate the game an 8/10. The character customization, gameplay, and build variety were amazing, but I definitely did not enjoy the Cathedral area.

P.S. You gotta try out the hot spring 😏
The favorite sibling compared to god eater 3 which didnt felt this polished, the gameplay was pretty fun but the story was pretty meh.

This is an amazing souls game that still holds up today. There is some parts that are little jank but I feel that is usually associated with most older souls games. The game stands out with the crazy amount of build crafting and the story is really good, Definitely worth a play through.

"Overall, this game still holds up and is super fun to play. It has some annoying moments but they're manageable. Hard to believe it's almost 7 years old! My favorite part has to be the character lore—hunting down vestiges to piece the stories together was a bit of a grind, but totally worth it haha."

Very fun souls like game, interesting mechanics and an engaging story with enjoyable side content that encourage backtracking. Some enemies can be a real pain, Bosses particularly like to true-combo punish the player. But what is a souls-like without a bit of luck involved in your journey. Pro tip, use a bayonet and shoot everything.

Great game for the price i paid! Lots of stuff to do and explore. Looking forward on the part 2 after this if that goes on sale lol. 
But overall solid gameplay, performance is good. 
Story is nice and good. 
No bugs so far. 
Screenshake option is missing which is really important for me. 

But still small things dont matter much. 
overall great experience!

My first playthrough of Code Vein was honestly pretty solid overall. The combat was fun and satisfying, the character designs were really cool, and I liked how much variety there was with the weapons and armor. The worldbuilding and atmosphere were also really well done and made the game interesting to explore.

One of the best parts of the game for me was the build diversity. There are a ton of different builds you can make, and the weapon and ability synergy feels really good when you start experimenting with different setups.

I do have some critiques, though. The hitboxes definitely need work sometimes because there were moments where I clearly dodged an attack and still got hit. Some boss mechanics were also a little too aggressive, especially when bosses constantly chase you down without giving much breathing room. And the Cathedral map? That place was honestly a headache to navigate through.

Overall though, after finishing the game and seeing the credits roll, I’d give Code Vein around a 7.6/10. I would definitely play it again someday to try a different build,

bought it on special thought it might be 50/50 if it good or not but its fun its an story based souls like game with awsome customisations played it before an long while ago but could not remember well how the game was fun tho

i played it one hundred percent of ps4 back in the day. decided to replay it today because i was able to get it for like 6 bucks. i dont know why theres so many negative reviews. i thought the game was god tier

The game itself is probably a 7/10. Bosses are fairly challenging, trash mobs are pretty trash. The sheer amount of different classes you have the option of learning and playing is impressive, even if the differences between the classes is nothing to write home about. For the simple reason that the game takes 10 minutes to load, every.single.time, I can't in good faith give this game more than a 5/10. It's a game that's worth playing if it's on sale, but that point of having to wait 10 minutes every start-up will test your patience.
like a dark souls game, but pretty easy in comparison. Would reccomend this first if you are looking into getting into souls likes or the souls games.

[h1] I feel like Code Vein was kinda easy for me…BUT!!!!! [/h1] 

As a souls-like player, Code Vein was really super easy (200 times deaths). But the main thing I like is the story (yeah every soullike has dark ending). It got me confused the whole time like [b]“what the hell actually happened? to lo and louis sister”.[/b] The game got 3 endings too, and I really like the way choices and objectives affect which ending you get best part (lo ending). Let’s hope Code Vein 2 gets even better (wait for big discounts 70%).

Soulslike (with boobs) but on the easier side if compared to other titles. 

Graphic was amazing for a game with anime graphic (those backshots scenes were amazing it made the characters looked cooler because they wear coats over their shoulders). 

Story is decent. But despite that, all characters got meaningful developments. 

Map designs were great. I dig the battles and exploration. 
Didn't experience the JP dub because the EN dub was good enough for me to enjoy the whole experience. 

8/10

I really wanted to enjoy the game. But unfortunately the game insisted upon itself way too much. The bosses are not hard in a fun way, but rather they attack way too fast + deal way too much damage. I had 2 bosses decide to be invisible because my framerate wasn't capped at 60fps and instead at 144. The healing was also slow and never enough to outdo any damage you may take. The story overall felt like it was told in a manner that was strained, and often featured "walking simulator" sections that often acted as exposition dumps about certain characters (often before you ever see them properly). Beyond that, the game feels clunky and unsatisfying. I also kept getting lag spikes during certain sections and I'm unsure as to why when the rest of the game did run quite well. However, I did like a lot of the designs of bosses and enemies, and a lot of the concepts in it are cool. I think the issue for me was the execution of these concepts.

Do you like getting lost on where to go after beating an area and having to wander around for hours to find some obscure path to the next location? Do you like janky combat or a nonsensical story and stupid looking gas masks on every character? If so, this is the game for you.

Can't get a refund on this turd game from Steam. Found out after beating a boss that if you don't have all of the "vestiges" you completely lose out on the chance to get that boss's Code to use that you were waiting for. Horrendous design decision, horrendous bosses.
Anime souls and I enjoy it.  Slightly easier than other souls games but thats ok

不建议任何一个本着类魂的人来游玩这个游戏 反弹手感一坨 怪全是神经刀 二周目拿脚填数值

这游戏唯一的好处就是可以捏角色和带队友 其他的跟白血教堂的引导一个完全是滚木
绝对的勾栏美术，看得我眼睛疼，四目的地图觉得自己老有巧思了
This Game is so good of Character design and Gameplay suitable for beginner of Souls-like and The music is so fire!
Anime souls-like Clone. Interesting enough for one playthrough, but not very memorable. Time is probably better spent in one of the From Software titles though.
It runs decent on Steam deck most of the time. runs better on an actual PC or Gaming Laptop
mediocre soulslike slop with zero interesting characters and clunky gameplay

- mixed opinion -

Launches perfectly almost instantly on my steam-deck with no configuration modifications

Fails to launch even with configuration modifications on my Desktop PC, arch-linux based with linux-zen kernel

hope this can be solved at some point as i would enjoy the game more on my desktop.

If you are using Steamdeck, Steam Machine in the future, or you play on Linux, don't bother buying it.
New hardware is not supported by the DRM, and you can get locked out for 24 hours by Denuvo.
some parts of the map are confusing and movements feels to stiff

3/10

The overall design is lackluster, the levels are like mazes, there are only five repetitive enemies throughout the entire game, and the plot is a childish, generic fairy tale.

Only desperate people - that is, anime/soulslike fans - will enjoy this.
Dark souls if the combat was mid, the story was mid, the character creation was amazing, filled with goons (overly sexualized characters) and a distinct lack of goons (average enemies to fight)
Type A and Type B character creation. Stop with this nonesense.
Need to choose either this game or Scarlet Nexus, this game doesn't disappoint me so far

I am writing this version of my review shortly after The Cathedral of Sacred Blood. 

The gameplay is pretty good. The difficulty rises relatively steadily, assuming you go to areas in the correct order (I didn't), until you reach The Cathedral of Sacred Blood. I got motion sickness from that area (I usually don't get motion sickness from constant spinning in games), the blood invasions are WAY too drawn out (The Player's Memories blood invasion is really fast in comparison), The difficulty of the Player's Memories is lower than that of TCoSB, creating a horrible difficulty pacing. The boss of TCoSB feels like a top-down rogue-like boss. Does not translate well into a game where you can't see behind you  at all times. 

I hate TCoSB, but as for the actual game itself, pretty good.

The soundtrack is decent, if there was a wider variety of tracks it would be better (having to listen to the same 2-3 tracks over and over as you improve vestiges is painful).

The Invading Executioner has like three attacks that start with the same or very similar animations as each other, making it near impossible to consistently dodge unless all you do with your life is play souls-likes and have mastered to art of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ off at the right time. Also has an attack that, if you are too close when it starts the attack, will kill you by stun-locking you.

As for the story, it's been awhile since I could not predict the plot of a game. It was very pleasant to have been surprised the way I was.

More than worth the buy on sale, and potentially worth it for full price. Especially if you really like souls-likes and/or have expendable money.

Update: Sometimes, in the 'receive valuables' menu when talking to allies, the game will get stuck and prevent backing out of the menu, forcing me to tab out and close the game. It happens regardless of how long the game has been running.

I played it only 3 hours at the time of writing this review, but it's a great Souls-like (with a bonfire-like mechanics where enemies respawn when you rest at it and you have to go back to the place where you died to regain your lost XP), albeit easier, and I like the modern and urban scenery that reminds me of Darksiders, another favourite of mine. Also it has anime-like graphics that even my daugther likes!

Had a really fun time playing CODE VEIN. It mixes challenging combat with great customization and a cool anime-style atmosphere.

• Fast and satisfying combat
• Great character customization
• Awesome visuals and soundtrack
• Flexible build system with Blood Codes
• Fun and memorable boss fights
• Companion system makes solo play more enjoyable

Definitely worth checking out if you enjoy Souls-like RPGs.
not the best souls, but it's worth a try. Pure mid.
Enjoyed for being able to play as a catgirl. Story/writing is really ♥♥♥♥ though.
decent soulslike, i just wish the 100% was not gated by an online achievement.

what can i say except it was a souls like

that means its good
